entries.title: Identity Element
entries.content: An element of a set that, when combined with any element under a binary operation, leaves it unchanged.
entries.example: 0 is the identity element for integer addition

entries.title: Identity Matrix
entries.content: A square matrix with ones on the main diagonal and zeros elsewhere, acting as multiplication identity.
entries.example: Matrix I * Matrix A = Matrix A

entries.title: Identity Relation
entries.content: A binary relation where every element relates only to itself.
entries.example: Equality relation on a set

entries.title: Image of Function
entries.content: The set of all output values a function can produce from its domain.
entries.example: Image of f(x) = x^2 over real numbers is non-negative reals

entries.title: Imaginary Number
entries.content: A complex number whose squared value is a negative real number.
entries.example: The number 5i

entries.title: Imaginary Unit
entries.content: The constant i, defined by the property that its square is equal to -1.
entries.example: Used to define the complex number system

entries.title: Implicit Differentiation
entries.content: A technique in calculus for finding the derivative of a variable when it is not isolated on one side.
entries.example: Finding slope of a circle x^2 + y^2 = 25

entries.title: Implicit Function
entries.content: A function defined by an equation relating two or more variables, rather than an explicit formula.
entries.example: Equation x^2 * y - y^3 = 5

entries.title: Improper Integral
entries.content: An integral where either the interval of integration is infinite or the integrand becomes infinite.
entries.formula: Integral from a to infinity of f(x) dx
entries.example: Evaluating integral of 1/x^2 from 1 to infinity

entries.title: Incenter of Triangle
entries.content: The point where the three angle bisectors of a triangle intersect, which is the center of the incircle.
entries.example: Incenter coordinates calculation

entries.title: Incircle
entries.content: The largest circle that can fit inside a triangle, tangent to all three sides.
entries.example: Drawing an inscribed circle inside a triangle

entries.title: Inconsistent System
entries.content: A system of equations that has no solution because the equations represent contradictory constraints.
entries.example: System: x + y = 2 and x + y = 5

entries.title: Increasing Function
entries.content: A function whose output values increase or remain the same as the input values increase.
entries.example: f(x) = e^x is strictly increasing

entries.title: Indefinite Integral
entries.content: The collection of all antiderivatives of a function, written with a constant of integration C.
entries.formula: Integral of f(x) dx = F(x) + C
entries.example: Integral of cos(x) dx is sin(x) + C

entries.title: Independent Events
entries.content: Events in probability where the occurrence of one event does not affect the probability of the other.
entries.example: Rolling a 6 and flipping a heads

entries.title: Independent Variables
entries.content: The input variables in a mathematical model or experiment that are manipulated.
entries.example: Time in a velocity-time graph

entries.title: Indeterminate Form
entries.content: An algebraic expression obtained as a limit whose value cannot be determined directly without algebraic manipulation or L'Hopital's rule.
entries.example: Limit of sin(x)/x as x approaches 0

entries.title: Index of Radical
entries.content: The integer n in a radical expression indicating which root is to be taken.
entries.example: In cube root of 8, the index is 3

entries.title: Indirect Proof
entries.content: A method of proof where the statement is proven by demonstrating that its negation leads to a contradiction.
entries.example: Proving that sqrt(2) is irrational

entries.title: Induction Step
entries.content: The step in a proof by mathematical induction where you show that if the statement holds for k, it holds for k+1.
entries.example: Completing induction proof of sum of first n integers

entries.title: Inequality Solving
entries.content: Finding the set of all values that satisfy an inequality statement.
entries.example: Solving quadratic inequality x^2 - 4 < 0

entries.title: Infimum of Set
entries.content: The greatest lower bound of a set of numbers, which may or may not belong to the set.
entries.example: Infimum of the open interval (2, 5) is 2

entries.title: Infinite Product
entries.content: The limit of the product of a sequence of terms as the number of terms goes to infinity.
entries.formula: product from 1 to infinity of a_n
entries.example: Euler's product formula for the Riemann zeta function

entries.title: Infinite Series
entries.content: The sum of the terms of an infinite sequence.
entries.formula: sum from 1 to infinity of a_n

entries.title: Infinite Set
entries.content: A set that is not finite, meaning its elements cannot be placed in a one-to-one correspondence with a finite set of integers.
entries.example: The set of all prime numbers

entries.title: Inflection Point
entries.content: A point on a curve at which the concavity changes from concave up to concave down, or vice versa.
entries.example: y = x^3 has an inflection point at (0,0)

entries.title: Information Entropy
entries.content: The average rate at which information is produced by a stochastic source of data.
entries.example: Calculating compression efficiency of text

entries.title: Initial Value Problem
entries.content: An ordinary differential equation accompanied by the value of the unknown function at a specific starting point.
entries.example: Solving kinematics equation given initial position

entries.title: Injective Function
entries.content: A function that maps distinct inputs to distinct outputs, also called one-to-one.
entries.example: f(x) = 5x is injective

entries.title: Inner Product Space
entries.content: A vector space equipped with an inner product, which defines angles and lengths of vectors.
entries.example: R^n with the dot product is an inner product space

entries.title: Integers Set
entries.content: The set containing all whole numbers, their negatives, and zero.
entries.example: -45, 0, and 989 are integers

entries.title: Integrability
entries.content: A function is integrable if its integral exists over a given domain.
entries.example: Continuous functions on closed intervals are integrable

entries.title: Integral Calculus
entries.content: The branch of calculus concerned with accumulation of quantities and finding areas under curves.
entries.example: Calculating total distance from velocity profile

entries.title: Integrating Factor
entries.content: A function used to multiply a non-separable differential equation to make it integrable.
entries.example: Solving first-order linear differential equations

entries.title: Integration by Parts
entries.content: A technique for integrating the product of two functions, derived from the product rule.
entries.formula: integral u dv = u*v - integral v du
entries.example: Integrating x * ln(x) dx

entries.title: Integration by Substitution
entries.content: A method for solving integrals by introducing a new variable to simplify the integrand.
entries.formula: integral f(g(x))*g'(x) dx = integral f(u) du
entries.example: Integrating 2x * cos(x^2) dx setting u = x^2

entries.title: Interior Point
entries.content: A point x in a set S is an interior point if there exists an open ball around x contained entirely in S.
entries.formula: exists r > 0 such that B(x, r) in S
entries.example: Every point in open interval (0, 1) is an interior point

entries.title: Interquartile Range
entries.content: A measure of statistical dispersion, equal to the difference between the third and first quartiles.
entries.example: IQR of dataset with Q1=10 and Q3=25 is 15

entries.title: Intersection of Sets
entries.content: The set containing all elements that are common to two or more sets.
entries.formula: A intersect B = {x : x in A and x in B}
entries.example: Intersection of {1, 2, 3} and {2, 3, 4} is {2, 3}

entries.title: Inverse Matrix
entries.content: A matrix that, when multiplied by the original matrix, yields the identity matrix.
entries.example: Solving matrix equation A*x = b as x = A^-1 * b
